idea-plugin.name: Opal Scripting Support
idea-plugin.description:
Developer-experience tooling for writing Opal scripts: plain .js files evaluated by Opal's GraalVM JavaScript scripting engine, which injects global proxy objects (client, player, world, renderer, notification, overlay, modules, mc, movement, rotation, inventory, esp, palette, and bound types like BlockPos / Vec2f / Color) into every script's scope at runtime.

This plugin does not implement a custom language or PSI layer for Opal scripts -- they are just JavaScript. Instead it leans on the JavaScript/TypeScript support already built into IntelliJ IDEA and WebStorm:

  • Ambient type definitions -- an opal-globals.d.ts file, scaffolded or refreshed into your project, gives the IDE's native JS engine full completion, parameter hints, and quick documentation for every proxy global, with no build step or import required.
  • Live templates ("Opal Scripting" group) for module registration, event handlers, settings (bool/number/mode/group), overlay islands, and command-palette views.
  • File | New | Opal Script scaffolds a starter script with working registerScript/registerModule boilerplate.

action.description: Create a new Opal scripting engine script (.js)
Copies or refreshes opal-globals.d.ts in the project root so the IDE can autocomplete Opal's scripting API
